title: Publish v1.0 publicly
question: How and when should Decision Explorer be released?
rationale: Ship v1.0 now: make the GitHub repo public and cut a tagged v1.0 release, with this project's own 48-decision log left in as the live example. The tool is feature-complete for a first release (capture, viewer, plan/audit flows, backstop, personalization, screenshot), so releasing now gets it in front of people rather than polishing privately. The decision log being public is intentional - it is the demo.

tradeoffs.note: feature-complete for a first cut; shipping now beats indefinite private polishing
the project's own decision log doubles as the demo, so the repo shows the tool working on itself
everything - decisions, _project.json, links - is now publicly readable; acceptable and intended as the example
delays getting real feedback; risks endless polishing
nothing public until everything feels finished (e.g. screenshot, more docs)
